_description: Architectural decision records: routing, state management, build pipeline, major library choices, cross-cutting refactors. Append only; supersede with a new entry instead of editing.

title: Shell wrapper, not SessionStart hook, for pre-prompt banner
context: Initial attempts (commits cc1586c..c1d3f54) registered a Claude Code SessionStart hook to print the banner. Five attempts (Node, POSIX sh, platform-dispatch, chafa-tiered viewport-fit) all produced interleave artifacts.
decision: Banner runs as a zsh function via shell alias (`alias claude='claude_with_banner'`) before the `claude` binary is invoked. Plugin entry: claude-bisio.plugin.zsh.
rationale: Hook approach is structurally broken: SessionStart is non-blocking, hook stdout is captured as model context (~1500-2000 tokens per fresh session), and /dev/tty writes race the ink-style TUI renderer. The wrapper executes synchronously before claude starts, so there is no race and no TUI fight.
consequences_positive: Zero token cost. No race. Clean banner placement. Cache-friendly (chafa output cached on disk).
consequences_negative: Shell-specific (zsh only today). No Windows native (WSL only). User must restart shell after install.

title: NN- prefix as canonical-membership gate for portrait variants
context: Picker and counter must agree on which PNGs in assets/bisio/ are canonical members of the Bisiodex. Renames and partial drops should not silently mis-order the dex.
decision: Files must be named `NN-slug.png` (e.g. `01-main.png`). The two-digit prefix is the membership gate; unprefixed files are silently skipped. Canonical set = (NN-prefixed PNG) intersect (positive `BISIO_WEIGHT_<UPPER_SLUG>` defined in bin/banner.config.sh). Source: bin/_pick_portrait.sh:18, bin/_counter.sh:201-211.
rationale: Filename-based membership keeps the picker variant-agnostic: drop a new png + add a weight, no code change. Two checks (prefix + weight) guard against half-applied retirements.
consequences_positive: Adding/retiring a variant is a one-file-and-one-config change. Picker and counter share the same gate.
consequences_negative: Asset filenames carry behavioral meaning; a rename without a weight rename silently drops the variant from the dex.

title: Chafa render cache keyed on PNG sha + flags sha
context: Chafa render is the slow path on first hit. Configuration changes (CHAFA_SYMBOLS, CHAFA_DITHER, etc.) must invalidate the cache without manual purge.
decision: Cache root: $XDG_CACHE_HOME/claude-bisio/v1-<png_sha>-<flags_sha>/<WxH>.ans. PNG sha is the first 8 hex chars of sha256 over the file bytes; flags sha is sha256 over the assembled chafa argv string. Both degrade to literal `nosha` if neither shasum nor sha256sum is on PATH. Source: bin/banner.sh:202-235.
rationale: Auto-invalidates on any meaningful change. Per-variant cache keeps first-hit-only slow path: collecting all variants warms 4 distinct cache entries.
consequences_positive: User edits banner.config.sh, saves, runs claude, sees new render — no manual cache busting.
consequences_negative: Cache root grows over time as variants and flag combinations accumulate. Acceptable: render outputs are tiny (a few KB each).

title: State persistence via XDG_STATE_HOME with mktemp+mv atomicity
context: Counter writes happen on every banner-rendered launch. Concurrent shell invocations or interrupted writes must not corrupt counts.txt or hall-of-fame.html.
decision: All state under ${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/claude-bisio/. counts.txt is plain KV (`slug count`, `__metadata` keys); HTML hall-of-fame is base64-self-contained. Every write goes through mktemp in the same dir, then `mv` rename. Source: bin/_counter.sh:220, 322, 372-379.
rationale: XDG compliance keeps state portable and out of $HOME root. Same-directory mktemp+mv guarantees same-filesystem rename atomicity. Self-contained HTML survives directory moves.
consequences_positive: Crash-safe writes. Easy reset (rm -rf the state dir). Hall of Fame is shareable as a single file.
consequences_negative: counts.txt format is line-oriented and not machine-validated beyond the awk regex `^[A-Za-z0-9_]+ [0-9]+$`. Manual edits can desync streak metadata.

title: Weighted-random variant pick via BISIO_WEIGHT_<UPPER_SLUG>
context: Variants need a tunable rarity model. Hardcoding probabilities in the picker would force code edits for retuning and prevent adding variants without code changes.
decision: Per-variant weights are positive integers in bin/banner.config.sh (`BISIO_WEIGHT_MAIN`, etc.). Picker awk does cumulative-weight selection. Weights need not sum to 100. First-ever banner forces 01-main regardless of weights (gated by `first-shown` sentinel) so the user meets the canonical Bisio first. Source: bin/banner.config.sh:55-58, bin/_pick_portrait.sh, bin/banner.sh:41-45.
rationale: Adding a variant: drop png + add `BISIO_WEIGHT_NEWSLUG=<n>` — no picker change. Plain assignments (not `:
`) make weights repo-level constants, not env-overridable.
consequences_positive: Variant-agnostic picker. Easy retuning. Test isolation possible by exporting weights in setup().
consequences_negative: PID-XOR-seconds seed (bin/_pick_portrait.sh:40) is good enough for cosmetic randomness, not for cryptography. Acceptable for this use case.

title: Hall of Fame as self-contained HTML with base64-embedded PNGs
context: On Bisiodex completion, render a celebratory artifact. It must remain valid if the user moves it, shares it, or the plugin is uninstalled.
decision: Render a single hall-of-fame.html under $XDG_STATE_HOME/claude-bisio/. PNGs are inlined as data:image/png;base64 URIs (one tile per canonical variant). No JS, no external CSS, no external image refs. Source: bin/_counter.sh:51-130.
rationale: Self-contained file is durable: the state dir can be moved, the plugin uninstalled, the asset PNGs deleted — the HTML still renders. Spec H7-H10 in BISIO_HOF_SPEC.md: no JS, no relative paths.
consequences_positive: Shareable artifact (drag into a browser tab on any machine). Survives plugin removal.
consequences_negative: File size is the sum of base64-encoded PNGs (~4/3 the binary size). Acceptable: 4 small portraits.

title: Forward paths if cross-shell pre-prompt banner is revisited
context: The current shell-wrapper is zsh-only. Three viable paths exist for cross-shell or in-CC banner support; documenting them so future contributors do not relitigate the same options.
decision: Three viable paths, in preference order: (1) Port the wrapper to bash / fish / PowerShell and distribute as a binary on $PATH (Homebrew, npm); only known way to get a reliable pre-prompt banner across shells. (2) Accept the token cost and emit the banner via Claude Code hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext — banner shows in transcript, not above prompt; ~1500-2000 tokens per fresh session, prompt cache amortizes within session. (3) Wait for Claude Code to add a zero-token UI-only channel (track anthropics/claude-code#11240).
rationale: Four other paths are explicitly rejected: re-trying /dev/tty writes from a SessionStart hook (structural race, see gotcha g002, g009), building a tiered-portrait system to fix placement (wrong layer, see g011), adding a PowerShell hook entry as a sibling to a sh entry without platform guard (CC-visible error, see g004), and waiting for plugin lifecycle hooks that do not exist (g010).
consequences_positive: Future contributors have a documented decision tree. Rejected paths are linked to the gotchas that explain why.
consequences_negative: None — this is forward-looking documentation, not an implementation choice.

title: Historical record of failed SessionStart-hook attempts (commits cc1586c..c1d3f54)
context: Before the current zsh-wrapper architecture (a001), five commits attempted a Claude Code SessionStart-hook approach. All were reverted. Documenting the attempts as a single ADR so the narrative survives the deletion of LEARNINGS.md and future contributors understand which paths have already been tried and why each one failed.
decision: Record the chronological attempt log: (1) cc1586c — Node hook writing to /dev/tty; failed: race; Node ~80ms cold start loses to TUI. (2) b54835d — rename to `evobaso@claude-bisio`; cosmetic only, install-command readability. (3) ed60151 — replace Node with POSIX sh (1-5ms fork); faster, still races TUI; duplicate prompt appeared. (4) c715f55 — collapse sh + ps1 into single platform-dispatch hook entry; removed `powershell not found` error on macOS, banner-cut issue remained. (5) c1d3f54 — four chafa-rendered tiers sized to viewport; visuals fit cleanly, but /dev/tty vs CC TUI fight unchanged. The current architecture (zsh wrapper, see a001) emerged after concluding that the fight is structural, not implementation-quality.
rationale: Constraints that drove the failures are captured as gotchas g001-g004, g009-g011. The commit log alone does not explain why each attempt was reverted; this ADR is the bridge.
consequences_positive: LEARNINGS.md narrative is preserved in structured form. Future contributors can run `git show <sha>` against any of these commits to see the actual diff that was tried.
consequences_negative: Commit SHAs may become hard to resolve if branches are aggressively pruned. Mitigation: SHAs are referenced; the tree at those SHAs is reachable as long as the repo's reflog or any tag retains them.
